roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 1: Scaffolding and build pipeline
roadmap.detail: Complete — Vite + React ES module build pipeline shipped in V2.0.0. Single App.jsx source, hashed bundles, deploy.sh automation, dev server with HMR.
Complete — CSS extracted to src/App.css and imported in main.jsx as part of the V2.0.0 Vite migration. No separate deployment required.

roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 3: Extract constants and utilities
roadmap.detail: Move ~30 constants (VENDOR_STATUSES, GUEST_GROUPS, PALETTES, SHIRT_SIZES, etc.) to src/constants/ and ~25 utility functions (formatDate, formatTimeRange, generateEventBriefHTML, computeHouseholdCounts, Hebcal fetch logic, color derivation, etc.) to src/utils/. Includes cleanup of var declarations in Hebcal fetch code to const/let and arrow functions. Import all back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session. Complete — 9 constants files and 9 utility files extracted. App.jsx reduced by 358 lines.
Move useSimchaSync, useDarkMode, and useSearchHighlight to src/hooks/. Import back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session.

roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 5: Extract shared components
roadmap.detail: Move small shared components to src/components/shared/: ArchivedNotice, ThemeProvider, GetStartedCard, PlaceholderTab, RsvpPill, CateringSummary, SuggestionsPanel, and any other reusable non-tab non-modal components. Import back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session.

roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 6a: Extract tab components (set 1)
roadmap.detail: Move Overview, Guests, Budget, Vendors, and Tasks tab components to src/components/tabs/. Each tab gets its own file. Import back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session.

roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 6b: Extract tab components (set 2)
roadmap.detail: Move Prep, Seating, Gifts, Favors, Accommodations, and Calendar tab components to src/components/tabs/. Each tab gets its own file. Import back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session.

roadmap.feature: Vite migration — Phase 7: Extract modal components
roadmap.detail: Move all ~20 modal and overlay components to src/components/modals/: VendorModal, HouseholdModal, DayOfOverlay, GuideModal, WhatsNewModal, ActivityLogModal, GiftModal, FavorModal, SeatingExportModal, GiftExportModal, FavorExportModal, TaskModal, PrepModal, TableModal, AssignModal, AccomEditModal, ImportModal, TimelineEntryModal, SearchOverlay, and AdminPanel/AdminLogin. Import back into App.jsx — no behavioral changes. 1 session.

Tasks promoted from Smart Suggestions carry sourceId and sourceCollection fields. Completing a task linked to an unpaid expense offers to mark it paid in Budget. Completing a task linked to an incomplete prep item offers to mark it Complete in Prep. Both paths use a confirmation modal with a Complete Task Only fallback. No silent cross-tab mutations.
Full multi-event platform: Create New Event provisioning UI, multiple event types, SaaS multi-tenant architecture, user authentication, and per-event billing.

Rebuild SimchaKit using Vite + React. The Babel standalone architecture causes real limitations. A proper build produces compiled, optimized output that still deploys as static files to the NAS. Phase 1 (single-file Vite scaffold) shipped in V2.0.0.
Configurable event timeline drives countdown clock and Overview display. Theme system with 9 color palettes applied app-wide.

roadmap.feature: Attending count override at household level
roadmap.detail: attendingAdults and attendingKids override fields on household records for when confirmed attendance differs from invited count.
Persistent dismiss mechanism for the task suggestions panel. A dismissedSuggestions list stored on the server so ignored suggestions do not reappear on every load. Currently known issue #4.
Inline chevron on each expense row reveals notes without opening the edit modal.

Dedicated ❓ Guide tab — last in the nav — with collapsible sections covering each tab, key workflows, and tips. Sections: Getting Started, Guests & Households, Budget & Vendors, Tasks & Suggestions, Seating, Gifts & Favors, Admin Mode, and a Quick Reference card. No server changes required.
Each household row has a status pill that opens a dropdown to change RSVP status without opening the full edit modal.

Collapsible Catering Summary card in the Guests tab. Three catering styles (plated, buffet-exceptions, buffet-headcount) set in Admin Mode. Meal choice breakdown table, kosher count, dietary requirements list with confirmed/pending badges, and Copy for caterer plain-text export.
Boolean outOfTown field on household records. Filter chip in Guests tab. Stat card on Overview. Drives the Stay & Travel tab.

New tab aggregating tasks, vendor payments, contract milestones, prep target dates, and event timeline. List view grouped by month, color-coded by source, with proximity badges and source-tab navigation. Month grid and click popover in V1.17.1–V1.17.2.
Progress tracker for Torah portion, Haftarah, d'var Torah, service prayers, and mitzvah project.
RSVP deadline field in Admin Mode. Guests tab banner with four visual states counting down to the deadline.
Export Seating modal with By Table CSV, By Person CSV, and Printable HTML view.
Table management with person-level assignments, capacity tracking, click-to-assign interaction, and unseated guest panel.

roadmap.feature: Seating tab — polish and task suggestions
roadmap.detail: Click-to-assign banner on table cards, mobile pill-tab switcher, summary line below stat cards, three new seating suggestion types.
Accommodation tracking for out-of-town households. Hotel block config in Admin Mode. Per-household notified/booked toggles, check-in/out dates, cut-off date warning, and task suggestion.
Computed suggestions from Budget, Vendors, Guests, Seating, and Favors data. Collapsible panel at top of Tasks tab.
Task records with category, priority, due date, notes, inline completion toggle, grouped category view, and filter/sort controls.

Contract Milestones section in VendorModal with inline add/edit/delete. Read-only list in VendorQuickView with proximity badges. Milestone task suggestions in the suggestions engine (within 60 days). Stored on vendor records — no new collections.
Vendor cards with contact details, financials, payment progress bar, contract URL, and VendorQuickView modal.
Admin Mode action that marks the event read-only. All data preserved and browsable. Reversible with password confirmation.

roadmap.feature: Budget view by timeline (By Timeline toggle)
roadmap.detail: Filter/group expenses in the Budget tab by which event component they belong to (e.g. Friday Dinner, Kiddush Luncheon, Evening Party). Adds an optional event section field to each expense.

Optional Budgeted field on each expense. Variance shown per line item (▲/▼ indicator), per category (bar marker + badge), and as a summary stat card. CSV export includes Budgeted and Variance columns. All variance UI hidden until at least one expense has a budget estimate.
Shipped in V1.21.1 — last contacted date and staleness indicator on vendor cards, Need Follow-up stat card, and filter toggle.
Standalone /simcha/ landing page listing all known events. Manually maintained. Bridges the gap until V2.0.
One-click JSON snapshot of all event data from Admin Mode → Data. Includes all collections plus adminConfig. Copy to clipboard with confirmation.
Configurable per-person label (Adult, Kid, Class) on favor records for vendors requiring categorization in their order form.
Favor tracker with configurable setup panel, size tracking, preprint/attending toggles, available-people panel, CSV export, and printable view.
Gift tracker with type, amount, date received, thank-you letter status, and CSV export.

🔍 header icon + ⌘K/Ctrl+K opens a search overlay spanning 8 collections: households, people, vendors, expenses, tasks, prep, gifts, favors. Grouped results with Show All, mobile bottom-sheet layout, Escape/✕/backdrop close. Row ID attributes (V1.15.1) and highlight on navigate (V1.15.2) ship as follow-on phases.

Collapsible card in the Budget tab. Auto-detects tippable vendors from expenses, calculates tips at 15/20/25% or a custom rate, supports per-vendor base amount overrides, and adds each tip as a Gratuities & Tips budget line item.
Shipped as Guide modal (📖) in V1.18.0 — 13-section in-app reference guide accessible from the header on all screens.
Save the Date and Invite Sent inline toggle buttons on every household row. Always-visible; accent-filled when sent. STD Sent and Invites Sent stat cards added to Guests tab.
Custom hex/HSL color input replacing palette selection. Auto-derives the 5 color roles from a single chosen color.

🖨 Print Brief button on the Overview tab. Generates a printable HTML document with event timeline, guest summary (with dietary requirements), confirmed vendors with payment status, and open/overdue tasks. Opens in iframe preview modal — print or save as PDF from browser.
Admin Mode action that wipes all planning data while preserving event configuration and admin password. Requires typing the event name to confirm.
If total table capacity is less than confirmed attending guests, the Overview tab flags it as a planning gap.
↑ ↓ buttons on each table card in the Seating tab. Order stored as a field on each table record. First/last buttons disabled. Exports respect custom order.
Collapsible per-vendor section on the Budget tab showing total contracted, total paid, and balance remaining.
Lightweight log of all data changes with timestamps. Especially useful when multiple family members edit simultaneously over WebSocket.
Shipped in V1.18.1 — Admin Mode Guests section: configurable Guest Groups, Meal Choices, and Sizes (shirt & pant).
Simplified read-only view for the day of the event: vendor contacts, confirmed headcount, outstanding payments, and a short manual checklist. Accessible from the Overview tab.
Contact log on household records matching the vendor contact log pattern — date, type, notes. For tracking RSVP follow-ups, address confirmations, and guest communication.

Collapsible 'Budget Insights' section at the top of the Budget tab using Recharts. Three charts: (1) Spend by category — horizontal bar with paid vs. outstanding split, sorted by total descending. (2) Payment timeline — upcoming unpaid expenses plotted by due date and amount. (3) Est. vs. actual — grouped bar for categories where a budgeted amount is set. Graceful empty states for all charts. Collapsed by default after first visit.

Collapsible 'Guest Insights' section at the top of the Guests tab using Recharts. Two charts: (1) RSVP status breakdown — donut chart (Invited / RSVP Yes / RSVP No / Pending / Maybe). (2) Group breakdown — horizontal bar by guest group showing adult/kid split per group.

Collapsible 'Task Insights' section at the top of the Tasks tab using Recharts. One chart: tasks by category — horizontal bar showing done vs. remaining split, sorted by total descending. Only shown when tasks exist.

EVENT_ID constant in App.jsx replaced with a runtime IIFE that derives the board ID from window.location.pathname. Each event folder automatically connects to its own data with no hardcoded values. Prerequisite for multi-event support.

roadmap.feature: New Event button in Event Picker
roadmap.detail: Add a '+ New Event' button to the event picker page (public/index.html). Requires a new POST endpoint in router.js to create the data file and public folder server-side, plus a simple form (event name, type) in the picker UI. Redirects to the new event on creation.

Admin Mode setting that requires a PIN before the event dashboard loads. Separate from the Admin Mode password (config changes) and the picker password (event list access). Allows sharing a direct event link with clients for read-only viewing while keeping the event protected.

roadmap.feature: New Event button in Event Picker
roadmap.detail: A + New Event button on the picker page. POST /api/events endpoint creates the public folder and copies dist/index.html. Includes Delete event capability. Requires picker access control to be in place first (shipped V2.49.0).

roadmap.feature: Multiple favors distributed at different sub-events
roadmap.detail: Currently each favor setup is linked to a single sub-event. Future enhancement: allow multiple favor types each linked to a different sub-event (e.g. water bottles at the party, kippot at the service).
Separate table sets and seat assignments per sub-event. tableId on person becomes tableAssignments: { [sectionId]: tableId }.

priorities.P1: Critical — needed before RSVPs arrive or payments are due. Blocking real use.
priorities.P2: High — significantly improves daily planning workflow. Should ship before the event.
priorities.P3: Medium — adds meaningful polish. Can be built in parallel or later.
priorities.P4: Future — platform-level or post-event. Long horizon.

knownIssues.detail: Resolved in V1.9.0 — the event timeline is now fully configurable in Admin Mode → Timeline. The hardcoded Sydney-specific timeline was removed and replaced with a dynamic admin-driven system.

knownIssues.summary: Admin Mode groups/sections/meals not yet configurable in UI
knownIssues.detail: Resolved in V1.12.3 — groups, event sections, and meal choices are now fully configurable in Admin Mode. The Guests tab reads from adminConfig with fallback to defaults.

knownIssues.summary: cityStateZip is a combined field and cannot be format-masked
knownIssues.detail: The cityStateZip field stores city, state, and ZIP as a single string. A proper format mask (e.g., enforcing ZIP as 5 or 9 digits) would require splitting it into separate city, state, and zip fields, which is a data model change. Deferred to a future version. Resolved in V2.40.3 — structured address fields (city, stateProvince, postalCode) replaced the combined cityStateZip field across all address entry points: households (GuestsTab), gift donors (GiftsTab), and vendors (VendorModal/VendorsTab).
knownIssues.resolution: Resolved in V2.40.0 — cityStateZip split into city, stateProvince, postalCode with country-aware UI.

knownIssues.summary: Dismissed suggestions reappear on every load
knownIssues.detail: Resolved in V1.13.1 — each suggested task now has a ✕ dismiss button. Dismissed suggestions are stored as sentinel records in the tasks collection, syncing across devices in real time. A 'Restore N dismissed suggestions' link appears at the bottom of the panel when any have been dismissed.

knownIssues.summary: Table cards give no visual signal when a person is selected and ready to assign
knownIssues.detail: Resolved in V1.6.3 — table cards now show a pulsing accent-colored 'Click to assign [Name]' banner replacing the type badge when a person is selected in the unseated panel.

knownIssues.summary: Seating tab two-column layout collapses poorly on mobile
knownIssues.detail: Resolved in V1.6.3 — on screens narrower than 768px the layout switches to a pill tab switcher (Tables / Unseated) driven by a window resize listener, eliminating the need to scroll past all table cards to reach the unseated panel.

knownIssues.summary: EVENT_ID is hardcoded to Sydney's event
knownIssues.detail: const EVENT_ID = 'simcha-sydney-mitzvah-2026' in App.jsx must be replaced with a generic placeholder and explanatory comment before GitHub publication. Deferred until app is feature-complete and active testing is done. Resolved in V2.46.0 — EVENT_ID replaced with a runtime IIFE that derives the board ID from window.location.pathname. No hardcoded value remains.
knownIssues.resolution: Resolved in V2.46.0. EVENT_ID is now derived dynamically from window.location.pathname at runtime. No hardcoded board ID remains in App.jsx.

knownIssues.summary: deploy.sh contains NAS-specific path in comments
knownIssues.detail: Lines 3-4 of deploy.sh reference '/volume1/web/simchakit/' which is a Synology NAS path. Must be replaced with generic '/path/to/simchakit/' before GitHub publication. Deferred until active use of the script is complete. Resolved in V2.48.4 — deploy.sh comments updated to use generic /path/to/simchakit/ placeholder. HOW_TO_DEPLOY.md updated to clarify no editing required.

knownIssues.summary: Event Brief button does not work in dark mode
knownIssues.detail: The Generate & Print button on the Overview tab stat card fails or produces incorrect output when dark mode is active. Works correctly in light mode.
knownIssues.workaround: Switch to light mode before generating the Event Brief.

knownIssues.summary: Mobile — Calendar tab hidden behind bottom nav bar when More drawer is open
knownIssues.detail: On mobile, opening the More drawer (which reveals additional tabs) places the Calendar tab behind the fixed bottom navigation bar, making it inaccessible without scrolling. The drawer content does not account for the height of the bottom nav bar.
knownIssues.workaround: Scroll up within the drawer to reveal the Calendar tab.

knownIssues.summary: Admin Mode section nav overflows horizontally on mobile
knownIssues.detail: The Admin Mode section tab bar (Event Details, Timeline, Guests, Accommodations, Theme, Tabs, Security, Data) renders as a single non-wrapping horizontal row. On screens narrower than ~640px the row overflows the modal width, requiring horizontal scroll to reach tabs beyond the visible area. Workaround: scroll the tab bar horizontally. Resolved in V2.48.3 — section nav container updated to flex-wrap: wrap so tabs flow onto multiple rows on narrow screens.
knownIssues.workaround: Scroll the Admin Mode section tab bar horizontally to reach hidden tabs.

auditItems.title: Scroll position does not reset on tab switch
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: When a user scrolls down on a long tab (Guests, Budget, Calendar, etc.) and then switches to a different tab, the window scroll position carries over instead of resetting to 0. This causes a large blank area to appear above the new tab content and the sticky header appears visually stranded in the middle of the screen.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Every tab switch resets the view to the top of the page. Verified by scrolling to the bottom of the Guests tab, switching to Budget, and confirming content appears at the top with no blank area above it.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add window.scrollTo(0, 0) inside the tab onClick handler in the tab navigation bar in App.jsx. The handler fires inside the tabs.map() render block when setActiveTab is called.
auditItems.do_not_break: Sticky nav bar behavior, active tab highlight state, any in-tab scroll positions (e.g. modal scroll areas)

auditItems.title: Countdown banner shows TBD venue with no way to set it
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The countdown banner subtitle prominently shows 'TBD' when no venue has been entered. There is no dedicated Venue Name field in Admin Mode — Event Details. The TBD either needs to be hidden when blank, or a Venue Name field added to Event Details so users can populate it. NOTE: The countdown banner already reads venue from mainEvent.venue (set per timeline entry). This item may be partially resolved — verify in a future session.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: When no venue is set, the subtitle omits the venue portion entirely rather than showing TBD. When a venue name is entered in Admin Mode, it appears in the countdown banner subtitle.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add a venueName field to adminConfig in AdminPanel.jsx. In OverviewTab.jsx (or wherever the countdown banner is rendered), conditionally render the venue portion only when adminConfig.venueName is non-empty.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing countdown banner layout, event date display, theme icon in banner

auditItems.title: Theme tab: add live mini-preview panel
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Theme tab in Admin Mode shows 9 color palette swatches plus a Custom picker, but users have no way to preview how a theme looks without saving and closing the panel. A small live preview panel inside the Theme tab showing the countdown banner, header, and a primary button rendered in the currently hovered or selected color scheme would eliminate the save-and-check cycle.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Hovering or selecting any palette swatch (including Custom) updates a preview area inside the Theme tab in real time. Preview shows at minimum: header background color, accent button color, and background tint. Preview updates before Save is clicked.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Theme section, maintain a local previewPalette state separate from the saved palette. Apply preview colors to a small self-contained mockup div using inline styles derived from the palette's CSS variable values. Do not affect the live app theme until Save is clicked.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing palette save/apply flow, Custom color picker and WCAG contrast logic, dark mode derivation

auditItems.title: Add Venue Name field to Admin Mode — Event Details
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Admin Mode — Event Details has no dedicated Venue Name field. This causes the countdown banner subtitle to show TBD. A Venue Name field (and optionally Venue Address) should be added to Event Details so it can populate the countdown banner and be included in the Event Brief print output. SUPERSEDED by audit-v2 finding: timeline entries already have a venue field used by the Event Brief and countdown banner. No new top-level field needed.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Admin Mode — Event Details contains a Venue Name input. The saved value appears in the countdown banner subtitle (replacing TBD). The value is included in the Event Brief print output. Field is optional — banner gracefully omits it when blank.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add venueName (and optionally venueAddress) to the adminConfig object in AdminPanel.jsx form state. Include it in the Save Configuration payload. Read adminConfig.venueName in OverviewTab.jsx countdown banner and in the generateEventBriefHTML utility.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing adminConfig fields, Event Brief generation, countdown banner layout

auditItems.title: Add tooltips to all icon-only action buttons
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Icon-only action buttons across the app (edit pencil, delete trash, calendar/STD toggle, envelope/invite toggle, etc.) have no labels and no tooltips. First-time users have no way to know what each icon does without trial and error.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Every icon-only button has a descriptive title attribute that appears as a browser tooltip on hover. Applies to: Guests tab row actions, Vendors tab card actions, Seating tab actions, Tasks tab actions, and any other tab with icon-only buttons.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add title='Edit vendor' / title='Delete expense' / title='Toggle Save the Date sent' etc. to each icon button element. These are in the individual tab files (GuestsTab.jsx, VendorsTab.jsx, etc.). No new components needed.
auditItems.do_not_break: Button click behavior, button styling, mobile tap targets

auditItems.title: Increase minimum password length to 8 and add strength indicator
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The current enforced minimum admin password length is 4 characters. For a tool containing guest PII (addresses, phone numbers, emails) and financial data, this is insufficient. The minimum should be raised to 8 characters and a password strength indicator bar added to give users visual feedback.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Password change form rejects passwords under 8 characters with a clear error message. A strength indicator bar (e.g. Weak / Fair / Strong) appears below the new password input and updates as the user types. Server-side validation in router.js also enforces the 8-character minimum.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx password change section, update client-side validation from 4 to 8 characters. Add a simple strength function based on length and character variety. In router.js, update the newPass.trim().length < 4 check to < 8.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing password change flow, forgot password / reset flow, admin login

auditItems.title: Archive vs. Reset: improve visual differentiation
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Both destructive actions — Archive Event and Reset Event Data — appear on the same scrolling Admin Data panel with similar visual weight, increasing the risk of triggering the wrong action. Archive should use a warning/orange style with a clear visual separator between the two sections.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: A horizontal rule or clear section break separates Archive and Reset. The Archive button uses an outlined orange/warning style with a warning icon. The Reset button retains its existing red/danger style. The visual hierarchy makes it immediately clear these are two distinct and separate actions.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Data section, add an <hr> or styled divider between the Archive and Reset blocks. Update the Archive button className or inline style to use var(--warning) or an amber color. No logic changes needed.
auditItems.do_not_break: Archive and Reset functionality, confirmation modals, server API calls

auditItems.title: Archive unlock code: add confirmation input and save-this warning
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Archive unlock code is typed into a single plain-text input with no confirmation step. Since this code cannot be recovered if lost (it is required to unarchive), users need a second 'Confirm code' input and an explicit warning to save the code somewhere safe before proceeding.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Archive modal shows two unlock code inputs: 'Unlock Code' and 'Confirm Unlock Code'. Save is disabled if they do not match. A warning message states 'Save this code somewhere safe — it cannot be recovered.' The confirmation summary shows the chosen code before archiving.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx archive confirmation modal, add a second confirmUnlockCode state variable. Validate that both inputs match before enabling the confirm button. Add warning copy inline below the inputs.
auditItems.do_not_break: Archive API call to router.js, existing unlock code logic, unarchive flow

auditItems.title: Next Payment Due stat card truncation fix
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Next Payment Due stat card truncates the vendor/expense name and amount with ellipsis (e.g. 'DJ/Entertainment — Balance · $8...'). This is one of the most action-relevant cards on the page — the amount should never be truncated.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Next Payment Due card never truncates the dollar amount. The expense name may wrap to a second line rather than being cut off. At all viewport widths, the full amount is visible.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In BudgetTab.jsx, find the Next Payment Due stat card render. Remove or relax any overflow:hidden / text-overflow:ellipsis / white-space:nowrap on the amount element. Allow the card to grow in height rather than clipping content.
auditItems.do_not_break: Other stat cards in the Budget tab grid, overall stat card row layout

auditItems.title: Budget EST vs ACTUAL color logic improvement
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The EST vs ACTUAL variance card displays under-budget amounts in a neutral/dark color, making it visually indistinguishable from over-budget at a glance. Standard data visualization convention is green for under-budget and red/orange for over-budget. The raw negative number should also be replaced with an explicit directional label.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Under-budget variance displays in green with label 'Under Budget' and a down arrow. Over-budget variance displays in red/orange with label 'Over Budget' and an up arrow. The sign of the number alone is never the only indicator of direction.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In BudgetTab.jsx, find the variance stat card. Add a computed isOverBudget boolean. Apply var(--green) or var(--red) color conditionally. Replace the raw number with a formatted label string.
auditItems.do_not_break: Budget stat card grid layout, other stat cards

auditItems.title: Calendar tab: persist List/Month view preference across tab switches
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: When a user switches to Month view and then navigates away from the Calendar tab, the view resets to List on return. The selected view should persist through tab switches within the same session.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Switching from Calendar to another tab and back preserves the previously selected view (List or Month). The preference resets on full page reload — session persistence only, no localStorage required.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx or CalendarTab.jsx, lift the calendarView state (currently local to CalendarTab) up to App-level state so it survives tab unmounting. Pass it as a prop to CalendarTab.
auditItems.do_not_break: Calendar tab render logic, List and Month view functionality, calendar filters

auditItems.title: Add success/confirmation toast notifications for all data mutations
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: When a user adds a household, marks a task complete, saves a vendor, logs a gift, etc., there is no visible feedback confirming the action succeeded. A lightweight 2-3 second toast notification at the bottom of the screen for all create/update/delete actions would significantly improve perceived quality.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: A toast notification appears within 200ms of any successful create, update, or delete action across all tabs. Toast shows a checkmark and a short description (e.g. 'Household added', 'Task marked complete', 'Expense deleted'). Toast auto-dismisses after 2.5 seconds. Only one toast is visible at a time.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add a global toast state (message, visible) to App.jsx. Pass a showToast(message) callback down to all tabs as a prop. In each tab's mutation handlers, call showToast after the updateData call. Render the toast div at the App level with a CSS transition for fade in/out.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing mutation handlers in all tab files, WebSocket sync behavior, modal close flows

auditItems.title: Empty state consistency audit across all tabs
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Empty states are inconsistent in quality across tabs. Some have a proper centered illustration and CTA; others have a plain text message or nothing at all. Every tab's zero-data state should have: a relevant emoji or illustration, a one-line explanation of what the tab does, and a primary CTA button to add the first item.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Audit all tabs for zero-data states. Each tab's empty state has an emoji, one-line description, and a CTA button. Verified tabs: Guests, Budget, Vendors, Tasks, Prep, Seating, Gifts, Favors, Accommodations, Calendar.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Review each tab file for the zero-records conditional render. Match the established pattern from the best-implemented empty state in the app. No new component needed — inline JSX per tab.
auditItems.do_not_break: Populated state renders, tab layout, filter bar behavior when empty

auditItems.title: Add populated/empty indicator dots to tab nav
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Currently only Budget and Tasks show numerical badge counts in the nav. No tab indicates whether it has any data configured. A subtle filled dot or checkmark on each tab once it has at least one record would let users see at a glance which planning areas have been started.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Each tab shows a small filled dot indicator when it has at least one record (e.g. any expense, any vendor, any gift). The dot is distinct from the existing badge count style. Tabs with zero records show no dot. The dot does not appear on tabs where a badge count is already shown.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx tab bar render, add a computed hasData boolean per tab (e.g. expenses.length > 0 for Budget). Render a small dot element conditionally. Style with a 6px filled circle in var(--accent-mid) or similar.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing badge count display on Budget and Tasks, tab active state styling, mobile bottom nav bar

auditItems.title: Vendor card payment progress bar: increase height and add label
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The payment progress bar at the bottom of each vendor card is approximately 4px tall with no percentage or amount label, making it nearly invisible and unreadable at a glance.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The vendor card progress bar is 8-10px tall. An inline label shows either the percentage paid (e.g. '15% paid') or the amounts (e.g. '$1,500 / $10,025'). The label is readable without expanding the card.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In VendorsTab.jsx, find the vendor card progress bar render. Increase the height style. Add a label div below or alongside the bar showing the paid/total amounts computed from linked expenses.
auditItems.do_not_break: Vendor card layout, expense linking logic, card grid responsiveness

auditItems.title: Seating tab: add capacity fill progress bar to table cards
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Table cards show capacity as a text fraction (e.g. '1/10') but have no visual bar indicator. A small progress bar showing seated/capacity ratio would make it easy to spot underfilled or overfilled tables at a glance across the full seating grid.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Each table card in the Seating tab has a horizontal progress bar below the table name showing seated count relative to capacity. Bar fills green when full, orange when over capacity. Already-present text fraction is retained alongside the bar.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In SeatingTab.jsx, find the table card render. Add a progress bar div below the capacity fraction using the same pattern as the vendor card progress bar. Compute fill percentage as Math.min(100, (seated / capacity) * 100).
auditItems.do_not_break: Table card assign/unassign flow, table card grid layout, table color theming

auditItems.title: OFFLINE indicator: add guidance tooltip
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The red OFFLINE header indicator gives users no guidance on what to do or what it means for their data. A tooltip or brief inline message should explain that changes will sync automatically when the connection is restored.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The OFFLINE indicator has a title attribute tooltip: 'You are offline — changes will sync automatically when your connection is restored.' On mobile, this same message appears as a small banner below the header when offline.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx or the sync indicator component, add title='You are offline — changes will sync automatically when your connection is restored' to the offline status element. For mobile, consider adding a dismissible banner that appears only when isOffline is true.
auditItems.do_not_break: Sync indicator display logic, online/offline state detection, saved label display

auditItems.title: Footer: hide Server Logs link outside Admin Mode
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The footer currently shows a 'Server Logs' link at full weight on every tab for all users. This is a developer-facing tool that should only be visible when Admin Mode is active.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Server Logs link in the footer is hidden when Admin Mode is not active. It becomes visible when the user is logged in to Admin Mode. The rest of the footer (version, event ID, Brook Creative LLC) is unaffected.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx footer render, wrap the Server Logs link in a conditional: {isAdminMode && Server Logs}. The isAdminMode boolean is already available in App.jsx state.
auditItems.do_not_break: Footer layout, other footer elements, Admin Mode login/logout state

auditItems.title: Guests tab: make 2A 2K shorthand self-explanatory
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The '2A 2K' guest count shorthand on household rows is compact but opaque to new users. A tooltip on hover or a small legend on the page would make it immediately clear what the abbreviations mean.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The '2A 2K' count display has a title attribute tooltip: '2 Adults, 2 Kids'. Alternatively, a small legend key (A = Adults, K = Kids) appears somewhere on the Guests tab. Either approach is acceptable.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In GuestsTab.jsx, find the count display element on the household row. Add title={`${adults} Adults, ${kids} Kids`} to the element. Optionally add a small legend below the filter bar.
auditItems.do_not_break: Household row layout, count display logic, mobile row layout

auditItems.title: Guests tab: make full household row header clickable to expand
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The expand/collapse chevron on household rows is small and easy to miss. The entire household header row should be clickable to toggle the expanded detail view, not just the chevron icon.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Clicking anywhere on the household row header (name, count, status badge, or any whitespace in the row) toggles the expanded/collapsed state. The cursor changes to pointer on hover over the entire row. The chevron icon is retained as a visual affordance.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In GuestsTab.jsx, find the household row header element. Move the onClick handler from the chevron element to the parent row container. Add cursor:'pointer' style to the row container.
auditItems.do_not_break: Expand/collapse state logic, row action buttons (edit, delete, RSVP toggle) — these should stopPropagation to prevent toggle firing when action buttons are clicked

auditItems.title: Calendar month view: reduce empty cell height
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: In Month view, each day cell is approximately 75px tall. Most cells are empty, making the calendar feel sparse and washy. Cells should be shorter by default and expand only when they contain items.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Empty day cells in Month view are approximately 48-52px tall. Cells containing items expand naturally to fit their content. The overall month grid is more compact and scannable.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In CalendarTab.jsx, find the month view day cell styles. Reduce the default minHeight from ~75px to ~50px. Ensure height is set to auto so cells with content expand naturally.
auditItems.do_not_break: Month view grid layout, item click/popover behavior, day number display

auditItems.title: Budget gratuity calculator: improve prominence
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Gratuity Calculator section is collapsed by default and uses a plain collapsible header, making it easy to overlook. Since this is a genuinely useful pre-event planning feature, it should be easier to discover.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) the Gratuity Calculator is expanded by default when tippable vendors are present, OR (b) the section header uses a more prominent button style (e.g. a filled accent CTA 'Calculate Gratuities') to draw attention. Either approach is acceptable.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In BudgetTab.jsx, find the Gratuity Calculator collapsible section. Option A: initialize the expanded state to true when vendors.some(v => v.isTippable) or similar. Option B: replace the plain header with a styled button.
auditItems.do_not_break: Gratuity calculator logic, collapse/expand behavior, vendor tip calculations

auditItems.title: Admin Theme Name field: add helper text explaining its purpose
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Theme Name field in Admin Mode (e.g. 'Sydney's Sweet Shop') is not obviously understood as a custom subtitle. New users may not know what it affects. A short helper text line below the field would clarify its purpose.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: A helper text line appears below the Theme Name input: 'This tagline appears below your event name in the app header. Use it for a theme name, hashtag, or fun phrase.'
auditItems.implementation_hint:
In AdminPanel.jsx, find the Theme Name form field. Add a small

or below the input with the helper text. Match the styling of other helper text in the admin panel.
auditItems.do_not_break: Theme Name save/load behavior, header display of the theme name

auditItems.title: Admin Notes field: surface it or remove it
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Notes textarea in Admin Mode — Event Details ('Any additional details about this event...') is not surfaced anywhere in the user-facing app. Users who fill it in may expect it to appear somewhere. Either display it on the Overview tab as an organizer note, include it in the Event Brief print output, or remove it to avoid confusion.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: One of three outcomes: (a) adminConfig.notes appears on the Overview tab as an 'Organizer Notes' section, OR (b) it is included in the Event Brief HTML output, OR (c) the field is removed from Admin Mode entirely. If kept, add label text: 'Internal Planning Notes (not shown to guests).'
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx, the field is in the Event Details section. Decision determines where to add the display: OverviewTab.jsx for option (a), the generateEventBriefHTML utility for option (b), or simply delete the field for option (c).
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing adminConfig save/load, Event Brief generation if option (b) chosen

auditItems.title: Admin Timeline: add tooltip explaining Main Event badge significance
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The star Main Event badge on timeline entries needs a short explanation of its significance — it drives the countdown clock on the Overview tab. Users editing the timeline may not realize what setting this flag does.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Main Event badge or its label has a title tooltip: 'The Main Event drives the countdown clock on the Overview tab. Only one timeline entry should be marked as the Main Event.'
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Timeline section, find the isMainEvent badge/toggle element. Add a title attribute with the explanation text.
auditItems.do_not_break: Timeline entry save logic, countdown clock derivation from isMainEvent flag

auditItems.title: Admin Guests: style Reset to defaults as secondary/warning button
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: 'Reset to defaults' and '+ Add Group' sit side by side with similar visual weight in the Admin Guests config section. Reset is potentially destructive. It should use a ghost/secondary style or subtle warning color to signal the distinction from the Add action.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The 'Reset to defaults' button is visually distinct from '+ Add Group' — either outlined/ghost style, muted color, or subtle amber/warning color. The Add button retains its primary style. A user scanning the UI can immediately tell which is the additive action and which is the destructive one.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Guests config section, find the Reset to defaults button. Change its className or style to match the secondary/ghost button pattern used elsewhere in the admin panel.
auditItems.do_not_break: Reset to defaults functionality, Add Group functionality

auditItems.title: Admin Guests: add placeholder text to Add Size input
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The size input in Admin Mode — Guests uses a CODE | Label format (e.g. AS | Adult Small) explained in helper text, but the input field itself has no placeholder. A new user staring at a blank field may miss the helper text.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Add Size input field has a placeholder attribute showing the expected format, e.g. placeholder='e.g. AS | Adult Small'.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Guests section, find the size input element. Add placeholder='e.g. AS | Adult Small' attribute.
auditItems.do_not_break: Size save/parse logic, existing size list display

auditItems.title: Admin Accommodations: handle long hotel website URLs gracefully
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Long hotel booking URLs overflow the Hotel Website input field in Admin Mode — Accommodations. Display should truncate with ellipsis and provide a 'Visit' link button to open the URL rather than trying to show the full string.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Long URLs in the Hotel Website field are truncated with ellipsis in display mode. A 'Visit →' link button opens the URL in a new tab. In edit mode, the full URL is editable in the input as normal.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In AdminPanel.jsx Accommodations section or AccommodationsTab.jsx hotel card, add an anchor element with truncation styles (overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, max-width). Render 'Visit →' as a separate link element.
auditItems.do_not_break: Hotel URL save/load, Accommodations tab hotel card display

auditItems.title: Overview tab: move Event Brief out of stat card row
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Event Brief Generate & Print card is an action, not a metric, but sits inside the stat card row alongside Guests, Budget, Tasks, etc. This creates a visual mismatch. The action belongs in the page header area, not the metrics grid.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Event Brief button is moved out of the stat card grid and into the Overview tab page header (top-right area). The stat grid contains only metrics. The button remains easily accessible.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In OverviewTab.jsx, find the Event Brief stat card in the grid. Remove it from the grid. Add a button to the OverviewTab header area (the section title row with the tab title). Pass the onGenerateBrief callback through as needed.
auditItems.do_not_break: Event Brief generation logic, Brief preview modal, stat grid layout with remaining cards

auditItems.title: Overview tab: Quick Notes textarea visual improvements
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Quick Notes textarea is functionally fine but visually bare — a flat rectangle on a flat background. A subtle inner border, light background tint, and an inviting placeholder hint would make it feel more intentional.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Quick Notes textarea has a visible inner border, a subtle background tint (different from the card background), and a placeholder text such as 'Your private planning notes — jot down ideas, reminders, and anything you want to remember.'
auditItems.implementation_hint: In OverviewTab.jsx, find the Quick Notes textarea. Update its inline styles to add border, background tint using var(--surface-alt) or similar, and update the placeholder attribute.
auditItems.do_not_break: Quick Notes save/sync behavior, notes content display

auditItems.title: Overview tab: Event Timeline inline Add Event shortcut
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Event Timeline card on the Overview tab shows scheduled events but has no in-context way to add a new one. Users must navigate to Admin Mode — Timeline. An inline shortcut link or button inside the Event Timeline card would reduce friction.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Event Timeline card header has a small '+ Add Event' link or button that opens the Admin Mode panel directly to the Timeline section. The link is only shown when the user is not in archived mode.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In OverviewTab.jsx, find the Event Timeline card header. Add a small button that calls setActiveTab('admin') or equivalent to open Admin Mode. Pass setActiveTab as a prop if not already available in OverviewTab.
auditItems.do_not_break: Event timeline display, Admin Mode open/close logic, archived mode restrictions

auditItems.title: Tasks tab: add item count to category group headers
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Task category group headers show the category name only. Adding a count in parentheses (e.g. 'Planning (8)') gives users a quick sense of scope without counting rows manually.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Each category group header in the Tasks tab shows the task count for that category in parentheses, e.g. 'Budget (1)', 'Planning (8)'. The count reflects the current filtered view (if filters are active, counts reflect filtered results).
auditItems.implementation_hint: In TasksTab.jsx, find the category group header render. Add a count derived from the filtered tasks array grouped by category. Display as 'CategoryName (N)'.
auditItems.do_not_break: Task category grouping logic, filter behavior, task completion toggle

auditItems.title: Seating tab: group Unseated panel by household
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Unseated guest list is sorted alphabetically by individual name. When assigning seats, users naturally want to seat household members together. Grouping unseated guests by household makes family units visible as a unit.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The Unseated panel groups people by household rather than listing individuals alphabetically. Each household group shows the household name as a header and the unseated individuals underneath. Households with all members seated are not shown.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In SeatingTab.jsx, find the unseated panel render. Instead of a flat sorted list, group the unseated people by householdId. Render a household header row followed by member rows. Use the existing households array from props to get household names.
auditItems.do_not_break: Seating assignment click logic, unseated count in stat cards, mobile pill tab view

auditItems.title: Seating tab: add rename prompt for default table names
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Tables default to generic names ('Table 2', 'Table 3'). The rename action exists via the pencil icon but is easy to miss. A soft placeholder prompt inside the card header for any table using a default name would nudge users to personalize.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Any table card whose name matches the pattern 'Table N' (default name) shows a subtle inline prompt: 'Click ✏ to name this table' in muted text below the table name. Tables with custom names show no prompt.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In SeatingTab.jsx, find the table card header render. Add a conditional: if the table name matches /^Table \d+$/, render a small muted hint element below the name.
auditItems.do_not_break: Table rename flow, table card layout, table color theming

auditItems.title: Calendar: align color legend with month view items
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The source-type legend (Events, Tasks, Payments, Milestones, Prep) uses colored dots, but month view calendar cells render items as plain text lines without the dot indicators. Either add a colored left-border or dot prefix to month view items matching the legend colors, or show the legend only in List view where it is actually used.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Month view items have a colored indicator (left border or dot) matching the legend color for their source type. OR the legend is hidden when Month view is active. Either approach is acceptable.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In CalendarTab.jsx, find the month view item render. Add a colored left border or dot using the same color map used in the legend. Alternatively, add a conditional to hide the legend when viewMode === 'month'.
auditItems.do_not_break: List view legend display, month view item click/popover, source type color logic

auditItems.title: Budget spend-by-category chart legend: improve size and placement
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The chart legend ('Darker fill = paid, lighter fill = outstanding, diamond = budget estimate') is in very small muted text below the chart title. This is the key to reading the entire visualization and deserves more prominence.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The legend uses colored swatches rather than text-only descriptions. It is positioned above the bars or as an inline legend to the right of each bar row. The font size is at least 12px. The legend is immediately visible without scanning for it.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In BudgetTab.jsx, find the category breakdown chart legend. Replace the text-only legend with swatch + label pairs. Reposition to above the chart bars or inline. Consider a flex row of colored squares with labels.
auditItems.do_not_break: Category chart bar render logic, budget tab layout

auditItems.title: VERIFY: Event Type conditional UI — completeness audit
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Some elements are already event-type-aware (header icon, Torah Portion card, Kippot stat card). This item is to audit whether any conditional elements are still missing. Auditor flagged this as fully unimplemented but that was incorrect — a completeness check is needed. FINDING: Partially implemented. Header icon and Torah Portion card adapt to event type. Kippot Needed and Kosher Meals stat cards remain visible for all event types — documented as intentional low-priority gap.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: A code audit confirms which elements are and are not already conditional on event type. Any missing conditionals (e.g. Kosher Meals stat card, specific tab visibility) are identified and either actioned or documented as intentional.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Search codebase for EVENT_TYPE_ICONS, adminConfig.type, and eventType references to map all existing conditionals. Compare against the full list of elements that should be conditional.
auditItems.do_not_break: N/A — verification only before any changes are made

auditItems.title: VERIFY: Venue field — confirm gap vs existing timeline field
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Before adding a new Venue Name field to Admin Mode Event Details (audit-004), confirm whether the venue is already capturable via the timeline entry for the main event. If the timeline entry has a location or venue field, the gap may already be closable without a new top-level field. FINDING: Gap does not exist. Timeline entries already have a venue field used by the Event Brief. audit-004 is superseded.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Review the timeline entry data model and AdminPanel.jsx timeline form. Confirm whether a venue/location field exists. If it does and already populates the countdown banner, audit-004 may be redundant or scoped differently.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Check the TimelineEntryModal in GuestsTab.jsx or its extracted file for location/venue fields. Check OverviewTab.jsx countdown banner render for how the subtitle is built.
auditItems.do_not_break: N/A — verification only

auditItems.title: VERIFY: Status badge theme-awareness — scope assessment
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Auditor flagged that the 'Deposit Paid' vendor status badge uses a fixed green/teal that doesn't match the app's warm palette. Before actioning, assess the scope: are all status badges using hardcoded colors, or only some? A single-pass fix may be worthwhile if widespread; isolated if only one badge. FINDING: No hardcoded colors. All badge colors use CSS variables and respond to theme and dark mode correctly.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Audit status badge color definitions across VendorsTab.jsx, BudgetTab.jsx, GuestsTab.jsx, and any shared badge components. Determine whether colors are hardcoded or already using CSS variables. Document findings.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Search for statusColors, badge, and color: '#' patterns in the tab files. Determine whether a global fix or per-badge fix is more appropriate.
auditItems.do_not_break: N/A — assessment only before changes

auditItems.title: FUTURE: Support multiple hotel blocks in Accommodations
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The current Accommodations tab supports only one hotel. Large events often have a primary hotel and an overflow or VIP block. Add a '+ Add Hotel' option to support multiple accommodation records, each with their own name, group code, cut-off date, phone, and website.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Admin Mode Accommodations supports adding multiple hotel blocks. Each block has its own card on the Accommodations tab. Cut-off date countdowns are per-block.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Requires changing adminConfig.accommodation from a single object to an array. Migration logic needed for existing single-hotel data. AccommodationsTab.jsx hotel card render needs to loop over multiple hotels.
auditItems.do_not_break: Existing single-hotel accommodation data, out-of-town household flags, notification tracking
auditItems.resolution: Implemented. adminConfig.hotelBlocks array replaces flat accom* fields. Migration from flat fields is automatic and non-destructive. AdminPanel Accommodations section supports add/remove blocks. AccommodationsTab loops over hotelBlocks with per-block cutoff banners.

auditItems.title: FUTURE: Event Brief modal preview before printing
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Generate & Print Event Brief button immediately opens the print dialog. An intermediate modal preview step would let users review the brief before committing to print, reducing wasted prints.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Clicking Generate & Print opens a preview modal showing the rendered brief. A Print button inside the modal triggers window.print(). A Close button dismisses without printing.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In OverviewTab.jsx or App.jsx, add a briefPreviewHtml state. On generate, set the HTML and open a preview modal (already exists as the brief preview modal lifted to App level in V2.10.0). Confirm current modal flow vs. this spec.
auditItems.do_not_break: Event Brief HTML generation, print output quality, dark mode workaround (known issue #9)
auditItems.resolution: Already implemented. The Print Brief flow opens a full iframe preview modal with Print / Save PDF and Close buttons. Acceptance criteria fully met as of the brief preview modal lift to App level in V2.10.0.

auditItems.title: Countdown banner gradient derives from active theme
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The hero countdown banner uses a fixed deep burgundy gradient. For non-Mitzvah events the palette may feel mismatched. Deriving the banner gradient from the active theme's primary color would make it automatically update when the theme changes.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The countdown banner background gradient uses CSS variables derived from the active theme palette rather than hardcoded hex values. Changing the theme in Admin Mode updates the banner gradient.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In OverviewTab.jsx countdown banner styles, replace hardcoded gradient colors with var(--accent) and var(--accent-mid). Ensure the ThemeProvider injects these variables correctly for all palettes including Custom.
auditItems.do_not_break: Countdown banner layout, text contrast on banner, custom palette CSS variable injection

auditItems.title: Exit Admin Mode button in app header
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Once Admin Mode is unlocked, it stays active until the page is refreshed. An explicit 'Exit Admin Mode' button in the app header when Admin Mode is active would make it easy to lock down on a shared or family device.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: When Admin Mode is active, a '🔓 Exit Admin Mode' button appears in the app header alongside Search and other header actions. Clicking it sets isAdminMode to false and returns to normal view.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx header render, add a conditional button: {isAdminMode && <button onClick={() => setIsAdminMode(false)}>Exit Admin Mode</button>}. Style to match existing header action buttons.
auditItems.do_not_break: Admin Mode login flow, admin panel open/close state, header layout

auditItems.title: Batch RSVP status update
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: RSVP status must currently be updated one household at a time. A bulk-select mode with a batch action dropdown would be useful when collecting verbal RSVPs en masse. Note: bulk RSVP was shipped in V2.4.0 — verify current scope before actioning.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Verify V2.4.0 bulk RSVP implementation covers this use case. If gaps exist, document them specifically.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Review V2.4.0 changelog entry and GuestsTab.jsx bulk RSVP implementation. This item may already be complete.
auditItems.do_not_break: N/A — verify before any changes
auditItems.resolution: Bulk RSVP shipped in V2.3.0 — per-row checkboxes, select-all with indeterminate state, sticky action bar, single audit log entry. Use case fully covered.

auditItems.title: FUTURE: Auto-seat by household assist
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: A one-click 'Suggest seating' helper that groups unseated guests by household and fills tables in household-sized chunks. This won't replace manual seating but gives users a useful starting point.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: A 'Suggest Seating' button appears in the Seating tab. Clicking it runs an algorithm that assigns households to tables respecting capacity constraints. The result is applied as a draft that can be manually adjusted. A 'Clear suggestions' option reverts to unseated.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Implement as a greedy bin-packing algorithm: sort households by size descending, assign each to the first table with sufficient remaining capacity. This is a self-contained utility function in src/utils/. Requires careful undo/revert design.
auditItems.do_not_break: Manual seating assignments, table capacity logic, seating export

auditItems.title: Configurable tab visibility per event type
auditItems.source: UX audit — Claude in Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Allow admins to manually toggle individual tab visibility. A backyard graduation party might not need the Seating or Stay & Travel tabs. A 'Visible Tabs' checklist in Admin Mode would let users hide irrelevant sections.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Admin Mode has a 'Visible Tabs' section listing all tabs with on/off toggles. Hidden tabs are removed from the nav bar and are inaccessible. The setting persists in adminConfig.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add a visibleTabs array to adminConfig. In App.jsx tab bar render, filter the tabs array against adminConfig.visibleTabs. Default all tabs to visible if visibleTabs is not set.
auditItems.do_not_break: Tab navigation, badge counts on hidden tabs, deep link behavior if a hidden tab is navigated to directly

auditItems.title: btn-sm touch targets too small on mobile (28px → 44px min-height)
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: btn-sm buttons render at 28px tall across the entire app — well below the 44px Apple HIG / Google Material minimum. This affects header action buttons, section action buttons (Import, Export, + Add), and the ← Events back link. The majority of touch target failures in the audit trace back to this single class.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile viewports (<640px), btn-sm buttons have a minimum height of 44px. Visual size (padding, font) may remain compact — the tap area is what must meet the minimum. Desktop appearance unchanged.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css, add a mobile media query: @media (max-width: 640px) { .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; } }. Verify this doesn't break any btn-sm used inside tight layouts like table rows.
auditItems.do_not_break: Desktop btn-sm appearance, table row action buttons, modal footer buttons

auditItems.title: Header icon buttons too small (28×28px → 44px tap area)
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The three header action icons (Search 🔍, Admin ⚙, More ⋯) are 28×28px — 57% below the 44px recommended minimum. On a real device these are frustrating to tap, especially one-handed.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Header icon buttons (.icon-btn in the header) have a minimum width and height of 44px on mobile. The visual icon size stays the same — only the tap area grows.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css mobile media query, add: .header-actions .icon-btn { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }
auditItems.do_not_break: Header layout, icon visual size, active state styling on icon buttons

auditItems.title: Gifts tab: 9-column table overflows on mobile — redesign as cards
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The Gifts tab renders a 9-column HTML table (FROM, TYPE, DESCRIPTION, AMOUNT, RECEIVED, ATTENDED, WRITTEN, MAILED, ACTIONS) that is 712px wide on a 400px viewport. Horizontal scrolling on mobile competes with page swipe gestures and is very difficult to use. This is the most critical layout failure in the audit.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile viewports, each gift row renders as a card showing the most important fields (From, Amount, Thank-You status) with secondary details accessible via expand. The full table layout is preserved on desktop. A single toggle could optionally switch between views.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In GiftsTab.jsx, add an isMobile state (window.innerWidth < 640, with resize listener). When isMobile, render a card list instead of the table. Each card shows: fromName, giftType, amount, thankYouWritten/Mailed status badges, and an expand chevron for secondary fields.
auditItems.do_not_break: Gift add/edit/delete functionality, export, thank-you toggle, search and filter

auditItems.title: Stat card labels too small (10px → 12px minimum)
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: ALLCAPS stat category labels (e.g. 'GUESTS INVITED', 'TOTAL BUDGET', 'KIPPOT') render at 10px across Overview, Guests, Budget, and Tasks tabs. This is below the WCAG-recommended 14px minimum for body text and is particularly hard to read on a glare-prone phone screen.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Stat card labels (.stat-label) render at a minimum of 12px. The ALLCAPS style can be retained but the font size must increase. Desktop appearance may also benefit from this change.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css, find the .stat-label rule and increase font-size from 10px to 12px. Check all stat-label usages across tabs to ensure no layout breaks from the size increase.
auditItems.do_not_break: Stat card grid layout, stat card content overflow, budget and overview stat grids

auditItems.title: FUTURE: Collapsible header on mobile scroll
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The sticky header is 87px tall on mobile — nearly 11% of a 768px viewport — and shows three rows of information. On a 375px iPhone this is even more acute. A scroll-aware header that collapses to a single row (logo + action icons only) after scrolling past the top would reclaim significant viewport space.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile, the header collapses to ~44px (single row: logo + icons) after scrolling ~60px down. The full header is restored when scrolling back to the top. Event name and theme name are shown only in the expanded state.
auditItems.implementation_hint: Add a useScrollPosition hook that sets a collapsed boolean. Apply a .header-collapsed CSS class conditionally. Transition the header height with CSS transition. Requires careful handling of the sticky app-nav offset (currently top: 60px).
auditItems.do_not_break: Sticky nav bar positioning, header action buttons, sync indicator, event name display
auditItems.resolution: Implemented scroll-direction listener using requestAnimationFrame throttle. Event name row (row 2) collapses via max-height/opacity CSS transition on scroll down past 60px, reveals on scroll up. Brand row and actions always visible. Mobile only (window.innerWidth < 640 guard).

auditItems.title: RSVP inline pill too small on mobile (20px tall)
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The inline RSVP status pill ('Invited ▾') on household rows is 66×20px at 11px font. The tap area is too small and the font is borderline illegible. This is a key action — changing RSVP status for a guest — and should have a minimum 36px height.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: The RSVP status pill on mobile has a minimum height of 36px and font size of at least 12px. The dropdown/popover behavior is unchanged.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css mobile media query, find the .rsvp-pill or equivalent class and add min-height: 36px, font-size: 12px, padding: 6px 10px.
auditItems.do_not_break: RSVP status change flow, household row layout, status color coding

auditItems.title: Header overflow menu (⋯) doesn't close on outside tap
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The header ⋯ overflow menu (Theme switcher, What's New, Guide, Day-of Mode, Activity Log) does not close when tapping outside it on mobile. Users must re-tap the ⋯ button to dismiss. This is a standard mobile interaction expectation.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Tapping anywhere outside the overflow menu dismisses it. Pressing Escape also dismisses it. The existing click-⋯-to-toggle behavior is preserved.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx, the showOverflow state already exists. Add a useEffect that attaches a document click listener when showOverflow is true, calling setShowOverflow(false) on any click outside the .header-overflow-wrap element. Use e.stopPropagation() on the menu itself to prevent immediate dismissal.
auditItems.do_not_break: Overflow menu item click handlers, ⋯ button toggle behavior, dark mode switcher

auditItems.title: Sync indicator: mobile label invisible — add compact text
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: On mobile, only the 7×7px sync dot is visible — the 'Live' / 'Saved Xm ago' text is hidden via .sync-label-desktop display:none. A 7px dot conveys almost nothing to users, and if it turns red (offline) the signal is very easy to miss.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile, the sync indicator shows a compact text label alongside the dot: '● Live', '● Offline', or '✓ Xm' for saved time. The mobile label is kept short (≤8 chars) to avoid crowding the header.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx, the sync-label-mobile span already exists alongside sync-label-desktop. Review what text is currently set on sync-label-mobile and ensure it is display:inline (not none) on mobile. The savedLabel object already has a .mobile property — verify it is being rendered.
auditItems.do_not_break: Desktop sync label display, header layout on mobile, offline/online state logic
auditItems.resolution: Resolved by moving sync indicator to footer entirely (V2.37.0). No mobile/desktop split needed.

auditItems.title: Modal side-by-side inputs should stack on mobile
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Two-column form layouts inside modals (e.g. City+State+Zip, Phone+Email side-by-side in the Add Household form) result in ~140px wide inputs on a 347px modal. This is too narrow for comfortable text entry and increases input errors.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Inside modals on mobile viewports, .form-grid-2 and similar two-column form layouts collapse to single-column stacked layout. Desktop two-column layout is preserved.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css mobile media query, add: .modal .form-grid-2 { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }. Check for any other grid-based form layouts in modals.
auditItems.do_not_break: Desktop modal form layouts, modal width and scroll behavior

auditItems.title: Modal footer buttons not full-width on mobile
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Modal footer buttons (Cancel, Next, Save) are inline-sized and left-aligned rather than full-width CTAs. On mobile, full-width buttons in modal footers are the standard pattern — they're easier to tap and communicate primary action more clearly.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile viewports, modal footer buttons are full-width (width: 100%) and stacked vertically (primary action on top or bottom, cancel secondary). Desktop inline button layout is preserved.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css mobile media query, add: .modal-footer { flex-direction: column; } .modal-footer .btn { width: 100%; }. Ensure button order makes sense (primary action last in DOM = visually bottom = thumb-reachable).
auditItems.do_not_break: Desktop modal footer layout, button click handlers, disabled states

auditItems.title: Search button shows ⌘K tooltip on mobile
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: The search button has title='Search (⌘K)' which shows as a tooltip on mobile long-press. The ⌘K keyboard shortcut is desktop-only and irrelevant on mobile.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: On mobile, the search button title is simply 'Search' without the keyboard shortcut hint. On desktop, the existing 'Search (⌘K)' title is preserved.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.jsx, the search button has title='Search (⌘K)'. This could be made conditional: title={isMobile ? 'Search' : 'Search (⌘K)'}. Requires an isMobile boolean in App.jsx (already available or easy to add).
auditItems.do_not_break: Search button click behavior, keyboard shortcut functionality on desktop

auditItems.title: Calendar list view: event titles truncate too early
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Calendar event titles in list view truncate prematurely on mobile — 'Confirm Kidd…' instead of 'Confirm Kiddush'. The card has room for more characters before needing to truncate.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Calendar event titles in list view show more characters before truncating on mobile. Ideally the title wraps to a second line rather than truncating, or the truncation point is extended to at least 30 characters.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css or CalendarTab.jsx, find the .cal-event-title style. Remove white-space: nowrap or increase the max-width available to the title. Allow wrapping rather than truncation if the layout permits.
auditItems.do_not_break: Calendar list view row layout, date and source tag alignment

auditItems.title: Bottom nav labels too small (10px → 11–12px)
auditItems.source: Mobile UX audit — Claude for Chrome, April 2026
auditItems.description: Bottom nav labels (Overview, Guests, Budget, Tasks, More) render at 10px. While 10px is common in native apps, paired with emoji icons it reduces legibility. 11–12px would be safer across diverse devices and vision abilities.
auditItems.acceptance_criteria: Bottom nav tab labels render at 11px minimum. Visual balance with the emoji icons is maintained.
auditItems.implementation_hint: In App.css, find .bottom-nav-label and increase font-size from 10px to 11px.
auditItems.do_not_break: Bottom nav bar height, tab layout, active state styling

rules.trigger: Bug fixes, copy changes, minor UI tweaks
New feature, new tab, or new major panel
Platform-level change — SaaS, authentication, database backend
